verses.1: Job again took up his discourse and said,
verses.2: “As God lives, who has taken away my right, And the Almighty, who has embittered my soul,
verses.3: For as long as life is in me, And the breath of God is in my nostrils,
verses.4: My lips certainly will not speak unjustly, Nor will my tongue mutter deceit.
verses.5: “Far be it from me that I should declare you right; Until I die, I will not give up my integrity.
verses.6: “I have kept hold of my righteousness and will not let it go. My heart does not rebuke any of my days.
verses.7: “May my enemy be as the wicked, And my opponent as the criminal.
verses.8: “For what is the hope of the godless when he makes an end of life, When God requires his life?
verses.9: “Will God hear his cry When distress comes upon him?
verses.10: “Or will he take pleasure in the Almighty? Will he call on God at all times?
verses.11: “I will instruct you in the power of God; What is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
verses.12: “Behold, all of you have seen it; Why then do you talk of nothing?
verses.13: “This is the portion of a wicked person from God, And the inheritance which tyrants receive from the Almighty:
verses.14: Though his sons are many, they are destined for the sword; And his descendants will not be satisfied with bread.
verses.15: “His survivors will be buried because of the plague, And their widows will not be able to weep.
verses.16: “Though he piles up silver like dust, And prepares garments as plentiful as the clay,
verses.17: He may prepare it, but the righteous will wear it And the innocent will divide the silver.
verses.18: “He has built his house like the spider’s web, Or a hut which the watchman has made.
verses.19: “He lies down rich, but never again; He opens his eyes, and it no longer exists.
verses.20: “Terrors overtake him like a flood; A storm steals him away in the night.
verses.21: “The east wind carries him away, and he is gone; For it sweeps him away from his place.
verses.22: “For it will hurl at him without mercy; He will certainly try to flee from its power.
verses.23: “ People will clap their hands at him, And will whistle at him from their places.
